concerns.text: FR-031b requires 'Ground truth MUST be established by human annotation independent of the pipeline' for validating inconsistency detection. However, the methodology only has access to reported numbers (p-values, effect sizes, sample sizes) without raw outcome data. Human annotators cannot independently verify whether reported statistics are arithmetically consistent without the underlying raw counts. This creates a circular validation problem: annotators reconstruct from the same reported numbers the pipeline uses, making 'independent ground truth' impossible. The idea's methodology sketch acknowledges this limitation but the spec requirement remains scientifically unsound.

T044 task description still contains '[deferred]' placeholder: 'when any domain exceeds [deferred] of corpus'. FR-027 explicitly requires 'no single source domain accounts for >30% of the total corpus'. The placeholder obscures traceability to the spec requirement and violates Constitution Principle VI (statistical consistency verification requires concrete thresholds). This is a regression from the claimed fix in the kickback resolution table.
